`.mdx` files cleanly.\\n- **`tsx`**: To natively run our TypeScript validation script as a pre-build step without 
compiling it first.\\n\\n### [Core Schemas]\\nWe will implement the provided Zod contract exactly as 
requested.\\n\\n#### [NEW] [content.ts](file:///C:/Users/pc/Desktop/EGY/New%20folder%20%2820%29/egyptian-awareness-libr
ary/src/lib/schemas/content.ts)\\nThe single source of truth for editorial mandates. This file will export:\\n- 
`SourceTier`, `CitationSchema`, `ReviewerSchema`\\n- `ContentFrontmatter` (including the `superRefine` blocks for 
Mental Health and Religion validation)\\n\\n### [Build Scripts]\\n\\n#### [NEW] [validate-content.ts](file:///C:/Users/
pc/Desktop/EGY/New%20folder%20%2820%29/egyptian-awareness-library/scripts/validate-content.ts)\\nA contentlayer-style 
Node script that:\\n1. Recursively scans `src/content/**/*.mdx`.\\n2. Uses `gray-matter` to parse the file 
string.\\n3. Passes the parsed frontmatter into `ContentFrontmatter.safeParse()`.\\n4. If validation fails, it formats 
the Zod error with exact file paths and line contexts.\\n5. Calls `process.exit(1)` upon failure, completely blocking 
Vercel CI deployments.\\n\\n### [Package Configurations]\\n\\n#### [MODIFY] 
[package.json](file:///C:/Users/pc/Desktop/EGY/New%20folder%20%2820%29/egyptian-awareness-library/package.json)\\nWe 
will add standard npm scripts to enforce this at the CLI level:\\n- Add `\\\"prebuild\\\": \\\"tsx 
scripts/validate-content.ts\\\"` (this guarantees it runs before Next.js builds).\\n- Add `\\\"validate:content\\\": 
\\\"tsx scripts/validate-content.ts\\\"` for local testing.\\n\\n### [Content Templates]\\n\\n#### [NEW]

hood). This is the only shared state primitive that survives the Vercel Edge Runtime AND scales to true concurrent 
users. Use it for: rate limits, MIST sessions, journal write-throughs, SRS scheduling, crisis interrupt counters,

commercial defaults.\r\n\r\nexport const limits = {\r\n  // 8 chat msgs / 60s per user — slows reflexive Layer-1 
spam\r\n  chat: new Ratelimit({ redis: kv, limiter: Ratelimit.slidingWindow(8, \"60 s\"), prefix: \"rl:chat\" }),\r\n  
// 4 OSINT debunker runs / hour — expensive, fights frivolous use\r\n  debunker: new Ratelimit({ redis: kv, limiter: 
Ratelimit.slidingWindow(4, \"1 h\"), prefix: \"rl:dbk\" }),\r\n  // 1 MIST attempt / 24h — the test must mean 
something\r\n  mist: new Ratelimit({ redis: kv, limiter: Ratelimit.slidingWindow(1, \"24 h\"), prefix: \"rl:mist\" 
}),\r\n  // Crisis: NO rate limit. Ever. (See B.3)\r\n};\r\n\r\n// Edge-runtime middleware enforcement\r\nexport async 
function enforce(\r\n  limiter: Ratelimit,\r\n  identifier: string\r\n): Promise<{ ok: boolean; remaining: number; 
reset: number }> {\r\n  const r = await limiter.limit(identifier);\r\n  return { ok: r.success, remaining: 
r.remaining, reset: r.reset };\r\n}\r\nWire it into middleware.ts so EVERY AI request passes through it before any 
model is called:\r\n\r\nCopy// app/middleware.ts\r\nimport { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 
\"next/server\";\r\nimport { jwtVerify } from \"jose\";\r\nimport { limits, enforce \n<truncated 248 
bytes>\nNextRequest) {\r\n  // 1) i18n — RTL/LTR direction based on accept-language and cookie\r\n  const lang = 
req.cookies.get(\"lang\")?.value || (req.headers.get(\"accept-language\")?.startsWith(\"ar\") ? \"ar\" : \"en\");\r\n  
const res = NextResponse.next();\r\n  res.headers.set(\"x-lang\", lang);\r\n  res.headers.set(\"x-dir\", lang === 
\"ar\" ? \"rtl\" : \"ltr\");\r\n\r\n  // 2) JWT verify (jose — only Web-Crypto JWT lib that runs on Edge) — pattern 
per Next.js 15 auth guide\r\n  const token = req.cookies.get(\"auth\")?.value;\r\n  let userId = \"anon:\" + (req.ip 
?? \"0.0.0.0\");\r\n  if (token) {\r\n    try {\r\n      const { payload } = await jwtVerify(token, SECRET, { 
algorithms: [\"HS256\"] });\r\n      userId = `u:${payload.sub}`;\r\n    } catch { /* invalid token → treat as anon */ 
}\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  // 3) Per-route rate-limit dispatch\r\n  const path = req.nextUrl.pathname;\r\n  let limiter = 
null;\r\n  if (path.startsWith(\"/api/ai/chat\")) limiter = limits.chat;\r\n  else if 
(path.startsWith(\"/api/ai/debunker\")) limiter = limits.debunker;\r\n  else if (path.startsWith(\"/api/mist\")) 
limiter = limits.mist;\r\n\r\n  if (limiter) {\r\n    const { ok, remaining, reset } = await enforce(limiter, 
userId);\r\n    res.headers.set(\"x-ratelimit-remaining\", String(remaining));\r\n    
res.headers.set(\"x-ratelimit-reset\", String(reset));\r\n    if (!ok) return NextResponse.json({ error: 
\"rate_limited\" }, { status: 429 });\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  res.headers.set(\"x-uid\", userId);\r\n  return 
res;\r\n}\r\nCopy\r\nThis pattern follows the verified Next.js 15 auth-in-middleware approach using jose for 
Edge-compatible JWT verification Authgear guide and the Upstash Edge ratelimit pattern Upstash blog.\r\n\r\n✅ DO — 
Make every AI route also re-check the limit server-side. Middleware fails open under load by design; the route itself 
must close the gate. ❌ DON'T — Use jsonwebtoken (Node-only) in middleware — it will not run on Edge Authgear. ❌ DON'T 
— Rate-limit the Crisis Interrupt route (B.3). Ever.\n</USER_REQUEST>\n<ADDITIONAL_METADATA>\nThe current local time

"content":"<USER_REQUEST>\nPART B — THE COGNITIVE KERNEL APIS (Shared by All Three Engines)\r\nB.1 Vercel KV + Upstash 
Ratelimit — The Concurrency-Safe Edge State\r\nYour stack includes @vercel/kv (which is now Upstash Redis under the 
hood). This is the only shared state primitive that survives the Vercel Edge Runtime AND scales to true concurrent 
users. Use it for: rate limits, MIST sessions, journal write-throughs, SRS scheduling, crisis interrupt counters, 
prompt-injection telemetry. The Upstash ratelimit library is the only HTTP-based ratelimiter designed for Vercel Edge 
Upstash docs.\r\n\r\nCopy// lib/kv/ratelimit.ts\r\nimport { kv } from \"@vercel/kv\";\r\nimport { Ratelimit } from 
\"@upstash/ratelimit\";\r\n\r\n// Sliding window — protects AI endpoints from prompt-injection & abuse.\r\n// Per the 
research's Layer-7 awareness: WE must not become an attention-economy actor.\r\n// So rate limits are TIGHTER than 
commercial defaults.\r\n\r\nexport const limits = {\r\n  // 8 chat msgs / 60s per user — slows reflexive Layer-1 
spam\r\n  chat: new Ratelimit({ redis: kv, limiter: Ratelimit.slidingWindow(8, \"60 s\"), prefix: \"rl:chat\" }),\r\n  
// 4 OSINT debunker runs / hour — expensive, fights frivolous use\r\n  debunker: new Ratelimit({ redis: kv, limiter: 
Ratelimit.slidingWindow(4, \"1 h\"), prefix: \"rl:dbk\" }),\r\n  // 1 MIST attempt / 24h — the test must mean 
something\r\n  mist: new Ratelimit({ redis: kv, limiter: Ratelimit.slidingWindow(1, \"24 h\"), prefix: \"rl:mist\" 
}),\r\n  // Crisis: NO rate limit. Ever. (See B.3)\r\n};\r\n\r\n// Edge-runtime middleware enforcement\r\nexport async 
function enforce(\r\n  limiter: Ratelimit,\r\n  identifier: string\r\n): Promise<{ ok: boolean; remaining: number; 
reset: number }> {\r\n  const r = await limiter.limit(identifier);\r\n  return { ok: r.success, remaining: 
r.remaining, reset: r.reset };\r\n}\r\nWire it into middleware.ts so EVERY AI request passes through it before any 
model is called:\r\n\r\nCopy// app/middleware.ts\r\nimport { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 
\"next/server\";\r\nimport { jwtVerify } from \"jose\";\r\nimport { limits, enforce \n<truncated 248 
bytes>\nNextRequest) {\r\n  // 1) i18n — RTL/LTR direction based on accept-language and cookie\r\n  const lang = 
req.cookies.get(\"lang\")?.value || (req.headers.get(\"accept-language\")?.startsWith(\"ar\") ? \"ar\" : \"en\");\r\n  
const res = NextResponse.next();\r\n  res.headers.set(\"x-lang\", lang);\r\n  res.headers.set(\"x-dir\", lang === 
\"ar\" ? \"rtl\" : \"ltr\");\r\n\r\n  // 2) JWT verify (jose — only Web-Crypto JWT lib that runs on Edge) — pattern 
per Next.js 15 auth guide\r\n  const token = req.cookies.get(\"auth\")?.value;\r\n  let userId = \"anon:\" + (req.ip 
?? \"0.0.0.0\");\r\n  if (token) {\r\n    try {\r\n      const { payload } = await jwtVerify(token, SECRET, { 
algorithms: [\"HS256\"] });\r\n      userId = `u:${payload.sub}`;\r\n    } catch { /* invalid token → treat as anon */ 
}\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  // 3) Per-route rate-limit dispatch\r\n  const path = req.nextUrl.pathname;\r\n  let limiter = 
null;\r\n  if (path.startsWith(\"/api/ai/chat\")) limiter = limits.chat;\r\n  else if 
(path.startsWith(\"/api/ai/debunker\")) limiter = limits.debunker;\r\n  else if (path.startsWith(\"/api/mist\")) 
limiter = limits.mist;\r\n\r\n  if (limiter) {\r\n    const { ok, remaining, reset } = await enforce(limiter, 
userId);\r\n    res.headers.set(\"x-ratelimit-remaining\", String(remaining));\r\n    
res.headers.set(\"x-ratelimit-reset\", String(reset));\r\n    if (!ok) return NextResponse.json({ error: 
\"rate_limited\" }, { status: 429 });\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  res.headers.set(\"x-uid\", userId);\r\n  return 
res;\r\n}\r\nCopy\r\nThis pattern follows the verified Next.js 15 auth-in-middleware approach using jose for 
Edge-compatible JWT verification Authgear guide and the Upstash Edge ratelimit pattern Upstash blog.\r\n\r\n✅ DO — 
Make every AI route also re-check the limit server-side. Middleware fails open under load by design; the route itself 
must close the gate. ❌ DON'T — Use jsonwebtoken (Node-only) in middleware — it will not run on Edge Authgear. ❌ DON'T 
— Rate-limit the Crisis Interrupt route (B.3). Ever.\n</USER_REQUEST>\n<ADDITIONAL_METADATA>\nThe current local time

cognitive audit, (C.4) the SM-2 spaced-repetition scheduler. Each maps directly to the 12 Cognitive Competencies in 
research §3.2.\r\n\r\nC.1 The FLICC + SIFT Library — Pure-TypeScript, Zero-Network\r\nThe FLICC taxonomy as 
established by John Cook is Fake experts, Logical fallacies, Impossible expectations, Cherry-picking, Conspiracy — and 
the five logical-fallacies branch expands into ~30 sub-fallacies (ad hominem, strawman, etc.) Cranky Uncle. The SIFT 
four moves (Stop, Investigate source, Find better coverage, Trace claims) is the user-facing protocol

"content":"<USER_REQUEST>\nC.3 Wayback + archive.today auto-snapshot (Prohibition #4)\r\nCopy// 
lib/provenance/wayback.ts\r\nimport { kv } from \"@vercel/kv\";\r\n\r\nconst SPN_KEY = process.env.IA_SPN_KEY!;        
// archive.org S3-style key\r\nconst SPN_SECRET = process.env.IA_SPN_SECRET!;\r\n\r\nexport async function 
saveSnapshot(url: string): Promise<{ url: string; archivedUrl: string; archivedAt: string }> {\r\n  // Idempotency: 
never re-archive a URL more than once / 24h\r\n  const cacheKey = `archive:${url}`;\r\n  const cached = await kv.get<{ 
archivedUrl: string; archivedAt: string }>(cacheKey);\r\n  if (cached) return { url, ...cached };\r\n\r\n  // Wayback 
Save Page Now API — pattern per archive.org SPN API documentation\r\n  const res = await 
fetch(\"https://web.archive.org/save\", {\r\n    method: \"POST\",\r\n    headers: {\r\n      \"Accept\": 
\"application/json\",\r\n      \"Authorization\": `LOW ${SPN_KEY}:${SPN_SECRET}`,\r\n      \"Content-Type\": 
\"application/x-www-form-urlencoded\",\r\n    },\r\n    body: new URLSearchParams({ url, capture_all: \"1\", 
skip_first_archive: \"1\" }),\r\n  });\r\n  const { job_id } = await res.json();\r\n\r\n  // Poll the job\r\n  for 
(let i = 0; i < 12; i++) {\r\n    await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 5000));\r\n    const status = await 
fetch(`https://web.archive.org/save/status/${job_id}`,\r\n      { headers: { Authorization: `LOW 
${SPN_KEY}:${SPN_SECRET}` } }).then(r => r.json());\r\n    if (status.status === \"success\") {\r\n      const 
archivedUrl = `https://web.archive.org/web/${status.timestamp}/${status.original_url}`;\r\n      const result = { 
archivedUrl, archivedAt: new Date().toISOString() };\r\n      await kv.set(cacheKey, result, { ex: 60 * 60 * 24 * 365 
});   // 1-year cache\r\n      return { url, ...result };\r\n    }\r\n  }\r\n  throw new Error(\"Wayback archival 
timeout\");\r\n}\r\nCopy\r\nThis follows the official Save Page Now API contract for authenticated archival Internet 
Archive SPN blog.\r\n\r\n✅ DO — Run this on a build-time hook for every external link in MDX content. Fail the build 
if any link fails to archive after 3 retries. ❌ DON'T — Trust your own server's first archival. Use it as your first 
witness only; serve archivedUrl to users so they can confirm.\n</USER_REQUEST>\n<ADDITIONAL_METADATA>\nThe current

"content":"<USER_REQUEST>\nC.4 MIST — Misinformation Susceptibility Test (Roozenbeek et al. 2023)\r\nThe validated 
MIST-20 / MIST-16 / MIST-8 instruments score from 0–20 (or scaled) with higher = greater veracity discernment Behavior 
Research Methods ScienceDirect.\r\n\r\nCopy// lib/cognition/mist.ts\r\nimport { z } from \"zod\";\r\n\r\n// Use the 
published 20-item MIST set under the article's CC license.\r\n// Each item: { id, headline, isReal: boolean, 
distractorType?: \"F\"|\"L\"|\"I\"|\"C\"|\"Cn\" }\r\nimport MIST_ITEMS from \"./mist-items-2023.json\";    // 
canonical set, version-locked\r\n\r\nexport const MistAnswer = z.object({ id: z.string(), answer: 
z.enum([\"real\",\"fake\",\"unsure\"]) });\r\nexport const MistSubmission = z.object({\r\n  answers: 
z.array(MistAnswer).length(20),\r\n  startedAt: z.string().datetime(),\r\n  finishedAt: 
z.string().datetime(),\r\n});\r\n\r\nexport function scoreMIST(submission: z.infer<typeof MistSubmission>) {\r\n  let 
real_hits = 0, fake_hits = 0, real_total = 0, fake_total = 0;\r\n  for (const item of MIST_ITEMS) {\r\n    const ans = 
submission.answers.find(a => a.id === item.id);\r\n    if (!ans) continue;\r\n    if (item.isReal) { real_total++; if 
(ans.answer === \"real\") real_hits++; }\r\n    else            { fake_total++; if (ans.answer === \"fake\") 
fake_hits++; }\r\n  }\r\n  const veracityDiscernment = real_hits + fake_hits;       // 0-20\r\n  const realNewsBias   
= real_hits / real_total - fake_hits / fake_total;  // distrust vs naïveté\r\n  const fakeNewsAccept = (fake_total - 
fake_hits) / fake_total;            // gullibility\r\n  // Vulnerability profile: which FLICC category did they miss 
most?\r\n  const missedByType: Record<string, number> = {};\r\n  for (const item of MIST_ITEMS) {\r\n    if 
(!item.isReal && item.distractorType) {\r\n      const ans = submission.answers.find(a => a.id === item.id);\r\n      
if (ans?.answer !== \"fake\") missedByType[item.distractorType] = (missedByType[item.distractorType] ?? 0) + 1;\r\n    
}\r\n  }\r\n  return { veracityDiscernment, realNewsBias, fakeNewsAccept, missedByType };\r\n}\r\nCopy\r\nThe endpoint 
stores results in KV and uses them to drive the personalized learning path described in research §3.3 Stage 
0:\n</USER_REQUEST>\n<ADDITIONAL_METADATA>\nThe current local time is: 
2026-06-06T23:09:51+03:00.\n</ADDITIONAL_METADATA>"}

92:{"step_index":4652,"source":"USER_EXPLICIT","type":"USER_INPUT","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-06-06T20:11:29Z",
"content":"<USER_REQUEST>\nC.4 MIST — Misinformation Susceptibility Test (Roozenbeek et al. 2023)\r\nThe validated 
MIST-20 / MIST-16 / MIST-8 instruments score from 0–20 (or scaled) with higher = greater veracity discernment Behavior 
Research Methods ScienceDirect.\r\n\r\nCopy// lib/cognition/mist.ts\r\nimport { z } from \"zod\";\r\n\r\n// Use the 
published 20-item MIST set under the article's CC license.\r\n// Each item: { id, headline, isReal: boolean, 
distractorType?: \"F\"|\"L\"|\"I\"|\"C\"|\"Cn\" }\r\nimport MIST_ITEMS from \"./mist-items-2023.json\";    // 
canonical set, version-locked\r\n\r\nexport const MistAnswer = z.object({ id: z.string(), answer: 
z.enum([\"real\",\"fake\",\"unsure\"]) });\r\nexport const MistSubmission = z.object({\r\n  answers: 
z.array(MistAnswer).length(20),\r\n  startedAt: z.string().datetime(),\r\n  finishedAt: 
z.string().datetime(),\r\n});\r\n\r\nexport function scoreMIST(submission: z.infer<typeof MistSubmission>) {\r\n  let 
real_hits = 0, fake_hits = 0, real_total = 0, fake_total = 0;\r\n  for (const item of MIST_ITEMS) {\r\n    const ans = 
submission.answers.find(a => a.id === item.id);\r\n    if (!ans) continue;\r\n    if (item.isReal) { real_total++; if 
(ans.answer === \"real\") real_hits++; }\r\n    else            { fake_total++; if (ans.answer === \"fake\") 
fake_hits++; }\r\n  }\r\n  const veracityDiscernment = real_hits + fake_hits;       // 0-20\r\n  const realNewsBias   
= real_hits / real_total - fake_hits / fake_total;  // distrust vs naïveté\r\n  const fakeNewsAccept = (fake_total - 
fake_hits) / fake_total;            // gullibility\r\n  // Vulnerability profile: which FLICC category did they miss 
most?\r\n  const missedByType: Record<string, number> = {};\r\n  for (const item of MIST_ITEMS) {\r\n    if 
(!item.isReal && item.distractorType) {\r\n      const ans = submission.answers.find(a => a.id === item.id);\r\n      
if (ans?.answer !== \"fake\") missedByType[item.distractorType] = (missedByType[item.distractorType] ?? 0) + 1;\r\n    
}\r\n  }\r\n  return { veracityDiscernment, realNewsBias, fakeNewsAccept, missedByType };\r\n}\r\nCopy\r\nThe endpoint 
stores results in KV a\n<truncated 1025 bytes>\nblish your population-level aggregated MIST results annually as the 
VCSA north-star (Prohibition is silent here — transparency is the credibility weapon, research §10). ❌ DON'T — Show 
users their MIST score as a leaderboard. That re-introduces Layer-7 engagement mechanics into your own 
product.\r\n\r\nC.5 SM-2 Spaced Repetition Scheduler\r\nSpaced repetition at 1d/3d/7d/21d intervals (research §3.4) is 
exactly what SuperMemo's SM-2 algorithm produces. The verified TS implementation is canonical 
open-spaced-repetition/sm-2-ts.\r\n\r\nCopy// lib/cognition/sm2.ts — pure, deterministic, dependency-free\r\nexport 
interface Card {\r\n  cardId: string;\r\n  ease: number;          // E-factor, default 2.5\r\n  interval: number;      
// days\r\n  repetitions: number;\r\n  due: string;           // ISO date\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function sm2(card: Card, 
q: 0|1|2|3|4|5): Card {\r\n  let { ease, interval, repetitions } = card;\r\n  if (q < 3) { repetitions = 0; interval = 
1; }\r\n  else {\r\n    if (repetitions === 0)      interval = 1;\r\n    else if (repetitions === 1) interval = 6;\r\n 
   else                        interval = Math.round(interval * ease);\r\n    repetitions += 1;\r\n  }\r\n  ease = 
Math.max(1.3, ease + (0.1 - (5 - q) * (0.08 + (5 - q) * 0.02)));\r\n  const due = new Date(Date.now() + interval * 
86400000).toISOString();\r\n  return { ...card, ease, interval, repetitions, due };\r\n}\r\nThis satisfies research 
§3.4's Ebbinghaus-replicated forgetting-curve schedule and turns every micro-lesson into a card whose return date is 
driven by the user's own recall performance.\r\n\r\n✅ DO — Make the scheduler the single source of truth for the 
user's \"What to do next\" home screen. The platform's calendar IS the cognition-building protocol. ❌ DON'T — Use 
streaks, badges, or push notifications competing for attention. Daily-streak gamification is the Skinner-box mechanic 
your research explicitly identifies as Layer 7 University of Michigan 
IHPI.\n</USER_REQUEST>\n<ADDITIONAL_METADATA>\nThe current local time is:

99:{"step_index":4659,"source":"USER_EXPLICIT","type":"USER_INPUT","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-06-06T20:11:57Z",
"content":"<USER_REQUEST>\nC.6 The DeepReal RAG — Pinecone Hybrid (Dense + Sparse BM25)\r\nFor the 70+ case-study bank 
(Piltdown, Wakefield, Stapel, Tuskegee, Cambridge Analytica, Pegasus, etc.), use hybrid search: dense vectors for 
semantic similarity + sparse BM25 for exact-name lookups. Pinecone supports both in a single index Pinecone 
docs.\r\n\r\nCopy// lib/ai/rag.ts\r\nimport { Pinecone } from \"@pinecone-database/pinecone\";\r\nimport { embed } 
from \"ai\";\r\nimport { model } from \"@/lib/ai/router\";\r\n\r\nconst pc = new Pinecone({ apiKey: 
process.env.PINECONE_API_KEY! });\r\nconst index = pc.index(\"cognitive-fortress\", undefined, { hostUrl: 
process.env.PINECONE_HOST });\r\n\r\nexport async function upsertCase(caseDoc: { id: string; text: string; metadata: 
any }) {\r\n  const { embedding } = await embed({ model: model(\"embed\"), value: caseDoc.text });\r\n  await 
index.upsert([{\r\n    id: caseDoc.id,\r\n    values: embedding,\r\n    sparseValues: bm25Sparse(caseDoc.text),    // 
computed via @pinecone-database/text or similar\r\n    metadata: { ...caseDoc.metadata, indexedAt: new 
Date().toISOString() },\r\n  }]);\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function retrieveSimilarCases(query: string, alpha = 0.6, 
topK = 6) {\r\n  const { embedding } = await embed({ model: model(\"embed\"), value: query });\r\n  const sparse = 
bm25Sparse(query);\r\n  // alpha = dense weight, (1-alpha) = sparse weight\r\n  const dense = embedding.map(v => v * 
alpha);\r\n  const sparseVals = { ...sparse, values: sparse.values.map(v => v * (1 - alpha)) };\r\n  return 
index.query({ topK, vector: dense, sparseVector: sparseVals, includeMetadata: true });\r\n}\r\nThis is the verified 
production pattern: text-embedding-3-small produces 1536-dim vectors that match Pinecone defaults OpenAI embeddings, 
and the sparse-dense hybrid is what enables you to retrieve \"Tuskegee\" by name AND \"1932 unethical syphilis study\" 
by semantics in the same query.\r\n\r\n✅ DO — Namespace by engine: deepreal-cases, mhgap-modules, religion-tafsir. 
Cross-engine retrieval is opt-in only. ❌ DON'T — Embed user-submitted text without their consent into the public 
index. Privacy-by-design.\n</USER_REQUEST>\n<ADDITIONAL_METADATA>\nThe current local time is: 
2026-06-06T23:11:57+03:00.\n</ADDITIONAL_METADATA>"}

hydration errors while maintaining component functionality.\n\n### 3. Accessibility (A11y) Violations\nSuccessfully

77:{"step_index":5547,"source":"MODEL","type":"SEARCH_WEB","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-06-06T21:31:25Z","content
":"Created At: 2026-06-06T21:31:25Z\nCompleted At: 2026-06-06T21:31:29Z\nThe search for \"\"Cannot find module './\" 
\"webpack-runtime.js\" \"next.js\"\" returned the following summary:\nThe error **\"Cannot find module 
'./webpack-runtime.js'\"** in Next.js typically occurs during the build process or when executing custom 
scripts[1][2]. It is often related to how Webpack resolves paths in different Next.js versions (particularly when 
moving from Webpack 4 to Webpack 5)[3][4][5] or when custom build scripts attempt to import internal Next.js files 
that are not meant to be accessed directly[2].\n\n### Common Causes & Fixes\n\n*   **Custom Build/RSS Scripts:** If 
you are running a custom script (e.g., to generate an RSS feed) that imports from `.next/server/...` or relies on 
internal Webpack paths, this error often arises because those paths change between builds[2][4].\n    *   
**Solution:** Avoid importing files directly from the `.next` directory. If you need to generate files (like RSS), 
consider using `next.config.js` to hook into the build process or run your script as a separate, standalone process 
that does not depend on internal Webpack runtime files[2][4].\n*   **Webpack Version Mismatch:** Upgrading Next.js 
(e.g., from v10 to v11+) involves a major upgrade to Webpack 5[3][4]. This can break custom Webpack configurations in 
`next.config.js` that rely on older, hardcoded paths or outdated plugin behavior[2][4].\n    *   **Solution:** Review 
your `next.config.js` file[2]. If you have custom Webpack rules, ensure they are compatible with Webpack 5[3]. Try 
commenting out custom Webpack configurations temporarily to see if the build succeeds, which will help isolate if the 
issue is in your configuration[2][4].\n*   **Deployment Platform Issues (e.g., Netlify):** This error has frequently 
been reported on platforms like Netlify when build caches become corrupted or when the `@netlify/plugin-nextjs` is 
outdated[6][7][8].\n    *   **Solution:**\n        1.  **Clear your build cache:** On Netlify or Vercel, use the
